title: iris pseudacorus var. acoroides
intro: Iris pseudacorus var. acoroides, also known as yellow flag, is a variety of the yellow iris plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and sword-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of iridin and other compounds. The health benefits of Iris pseudacorus var. acoroides include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to act as a diuretic and anti-spasmodic. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tinctures, infusions, and ointments, and its root can be used to make a yellow dye.
